highlights.text: I’ll be watching the reaction here though and if we can flush it and recover with momentum (ideally down to 5600) I’d long.” Today at 10am, we got exactly this and we flushed down to 5600 to the tick, recovered 5620, and squeezed 70 points Back on Friday I stated: “My general lean is that ES can do some backtesting on Monday.

As readers know I frequently talk about how in the context of a downtrend ES does two things: It goes “elevator down” and then it “short squeezes” and each of those correlates to one of my three setup types.
On Friday morning, we lost it, Breakdown Shorts were triggered, and we went “elevator down” for nearly 200 points.
After “Elevator Down” sells however comes violent short squeezes and short squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.

A Failed Breakdown here would be very much of interest and this remains a strong zone .” At 10am Monday we flushed 5565 (March 13th low), put in a big wick down to 5536, and recovered to trigger a monster 150+ point squeeze.
For those who missed that squeeze though, today delivered another Failed Breakdown.
Since bears control I am in no rush to insta-buy this or any support (my preference is always high quality Failed Breakdowns).
In today's newsletter I'll talk this, I'll discuss yesterday's Failed Breakdown in detail as its an excellent example of my core setup.

post_title: "Tariff Day" Tomorrow. Can SPX Bulls Keep The Rally Going? April 2nd Plan
